[Theory: The claim "One can crack their teeth biting a Pecan" is likely incorrect. Pecans are hard and have a tough outer shell, which makes them difficult to bite without causing damage to the mouth. The act of biting a pecan would likely cause pain or damage to the teeth, especially if the person is not used to it. The idea of cracking teeth by biting a pecan is not common sense, as it would be difficult to do without causing harm. The claim is not supported by human common sense.]
